description: Manually defined range-bound/mean-reverting market windows. NOT from Lo & MacKinlay (1988) — that paper provides the variance ratio test methodology, not specific choppy date ranges. These windows are from SPY price charts showing trendless whipsaw periods.
dateSource: SPY price history showing range-bound periods with failed breakouts, identified by visual inspection.

eventWindows.label: 2011 H2: SPY range-bound 1100-1290, multiple failed breakouts
2015-2016: SPY whipsawed 2000-2100, China deval + oil crash chop

passCriterion: Detector fires within 10 trading days of window start. No coverage requirement — choppy is hard to pin precisely.
